ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Nov-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Freedman, via ZOOM, for Dagmar Prudlow, Beneficiary, Objector; Matthew Schauer, via ZOOM, for Patricia Costello and Robert Noelke, Petitioners.

The court notes that the original version of this petition, filed in September of 2020, has been on calendar nine times since. Mr. Schauer is reminded that both of his client's signatures are require for verification and points to the amended petition as an example and reminds him that the court has notified him of the same issue multiple times. The court then turns to the proof submitted regarding payment to the IRS which is merely a photocopy of the check made payable to the IRS which does not constitute proof that the IRS has received payment. The court then points to the due date on the IRS statement and asks if that the amount is even up to date. Mr. Schauer reports that the CPA had issues contacting the IRS and was unable to confirm the balance owed and states that the Executors are ready to pay the outstanding balance, if needed. The court advises that it expects the Executors to pay any supplemental bill as soon as it's received. Mr. Freedman points to how frustrating it has been for his client regarding the delay with the IRS appeal process. The court then queries Mr. Schauer regarding which financial institution holds the mystery certificate ending in numbers 4445. Mr. Schauer is uncertain and advises that he will confirm the preceding with his clients. The court reminds Mr. Schauer that it expects him to know which institutions hold the estate's money as part of his accounting. Mr. Freedman states that he doesn't believe that the Co-Executors can comply with their obligations and asks that the court issue an Order to Show Cause to bring accounting current and to set deadlines for a preliminary distribution. The court then advises Mr. Schauer that his reply regarding the reverse mortgage is unacceptable and that instead of referring to the letter of special administration, the court is expecting proof of the money owed to and paid to the mortgage company. The court reminds Mr. Schauer that moving forward, it expects prompt reply to Examiner's notes.

The Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

The court begins by querying counsel if the amount being claimed by the IRS has been paid. Petition For Reinstatement Of Suspended Executors
Off Calendar Dec-07-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Loren Lopin appeared by zoom for Nicholas Kaftan (Attorney-in-Fact for Dagmar Prudio).
Holger Siegwart appeared by zoom for Robert Noelke and Patricia Costello (Executors).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not the petition to reinstate Mr. Siegwart's Client's is a request for reconsideration, whether it needs to be decided by Judge Wiss, whether it requires an evidentiary hearing, whether it is appropriate to appoint Mr. Vaught when Mr. Siegwart's Clients have not been removed, have not resigned, have not died and still have priority, etc. Discussion was also held with regard to the allegations of Mr. Lopin having lied to the Court and having misrepresented facts, Mr. Lopin's failure to respond to such allegations, the issue of whether or not there is any urgency to address the German taxes, correspondence that was received from the German CPA, the manner in which Mr. Lopin had his Client sign documents under penalty of perjury (under German Law), Mr. Vaught not being familiar with German tax law, etc. Upon conclusion of argument/discussion, the Court granted the Petition for Reinstatement of Suspended Executors and denied the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-09-2020 Continued To Nov-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Loren Lopin appeared by zoom for Nicholas Kaftan (Attorney-in-Fact for Dagmar Prudio).
Jon Vaught appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Holger Siegwart appeared in Court for Robert Noelke and Patricia Costello (Executors).

Mr. Siegwart stated that the matter is not in a position to have a successor administrator appointed, that the Probate Code does not allow appointment of a successor unless there is a vacancy, that Judge Wiss was duped by Mr. Lopin and that Mr. Lopin lied about the German tax laws in Court. Mr. Siegwart also stated that the Court should reverse the order suspending his Clients on its own motion, that they had a settlement to have one of the Executors reinstated and handle everything, that Mr. Kaftan walked away from the settlement and that nothing has happened. In addition, Mr. Siegwart stated that having Mr. Vaught appointed will incur additional costs to the estate, that there is no evidence for removal and no grounds for removal, and that the issue of the tax due to be paid in Germany is not a reason for removal. Mr. Lopin stated that he has no intention of responding to Mr. Siegwart's comments, that his petition is what's before the Court, that there is no petition to reinstate the Executors and that that there is a vacancy because the Executors are suspended. Mr. Lopin also stated that there is no reason not to appoint Mr. Vaught, that there are no objections by the suspended Executors to the appointment of Mr. Vaught, that right now the Administrator needs to pay property taxes and collect rents, that the Client in Germany is now negotiating with the German tax office and that the German tax office does not understand why this is taking so long. In addition, Mr. Lopin stated that his Client has been paying the German taxes out of her own money, that this is putting his Client under tremendous stress, that they need to file a fiduciary tax return, that the tenants need to be managed and that they need to sell the real estate to pay the German taxes. Furthermore, Mr. Lopin stated that there isn't enough liquidity to pay the 30% tax that is due in Germany, did not provide the Court with a due date of the German tax, stated that the German tax office would like to see this closed before the end of this year, that two years ago payment was made to hold the tax office off and that they are now coming back. Mr. Lopin also went on to say that he hasn't seen a fiduciary tax return, that he does not know if the 2019 taxes were paid, that he does not know that a tax return needs to be amended, that the German CPA firm will not work with Mr. Siegwart's Client, that the German tax due is $1,700,000.00, that there is about $400,000.00 right now and that unless one piece of real property is sold, they will not get to that number. Further discussion was held with regard to advancing the petition to reinstate, the conflicting report from Mr. Lopin regarding the German tax being due and now indicating that they are negotiating, how the rents are currently being handled, the York Street property being sold to cover the taxes, Mr. Siegwart's attempts to communicate with Mr. Lopin and not receiving any response, etc. The Court stated that it is not prepared to grant the petition, but it is willing to appoint Mr. Vaught as the special administrator with specific powers. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to November 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Lopin to amend the petition for special administration that indicates the specific acts that are to be immediately addressed by Mr. Vaught. The hearing of February 10, 2021 on the petition to reinstate Executors is advanced to January 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-31-2020 Continued To Sep-28-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matthew Schauer appeared by zoom for Robert Noelke and Patricia Costello.
Loren Lopin appeared by telephone for and with Nicolas Kaftan by zoom.

The Court noted that Mr. Kaftan filed a declaration raising serious concerns, and expressed concern stating that there is no response to the extensive Probate Examiner's notes. Mr. Schauer apologized to the Court stating that the issues listed in the Examiner's notes are of his own fault and not the Executors, that he made some mistakes and that he will meet with Mr. Kaftan. Mr. Lopin stated that his Client flew in from Germany on November 26, 2018 for a meeting in his office, that the meeting was cancelled by Mr. Siegwart, and that they refused to meet with him and his Client. Mr. Lopin also stated that his biggest concern is the German tax, that the German Tax Authorities is imposing a 30% tax and interest and a penalty, that Dagmar needs to come up with 1.7 million dollars and that there is no extension on the 706 taxes which is close to $40,000.00. In addition, Mr. Lopin stated that there is concern that one of the Executors took a kickback on the sale of the property, that there was no notice of proposed action of the sale of the property, that there was no following of the Court rules and that he would like to see the Executors removed. Mr. Schauer stated that he does not know what to do and that the Executors are not bonded. The Court stated that it is very concerned. Mr. Lopin stated that immediate action is needed, that there are three properties, that one property has no tenants, that two of the properties have tenants with no rent being paid, that the estate is losing over $10,000.00 a month and that the Executors are not competent. The Court suspends the Co-Executors immediately and orders the Co-Executors to immediately account. Mr. Lopin is to come in ex parte for appointment of a temporary Executor and file a petition for general appointment. The Court will consider penalties. The First and Final Account and Report of Executors, etc. is continued to September 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the immediate account is also set for September 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Continued To Apr-01-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matthew Schauer appeared by telephone for Administrator.
Lorin Lopen for Nicolas Kaftan Attorney-in-Fact for Dagmar Prudlo.

Mr. Schauer stated that there is a lingering tax issue, that it is a dispute over penalties and late filing fees, that there are $39,000.00 in fees, and that if they can't get it overturned, they will go after the Executor's fees. Mr. Schauer also stated that the Executor would like to distribute as much as they could now and hold back funds for those fees. The Court stated that it wants an inventory and appraisal, and that it does not have a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration, or a petition for preliminary distribution. Mr. Schauer stated that he thought all of the inventories and appraisals had been filed. Mr. Lopen stated that one property was sold, that the Executor used her Partner for the listing and that the Partner kicked back a finder's fee. Mr. Lopen also stated that one property is vacant, that rent is not being paid for the other property, that the new estate property tax is being re-assessed, that Dagmar wants the properties to be sold, that she does not trust that the Attorney and the Executors will do that, and that his Client came out from Germany and the Attorney declined to meet with his Client. In addition, Mr. Lopen stated that his Client wants the Executors removed and have her own Executor, that the German tax authorities are coming after his Client and requested that a final petition be filed in 30-days. The Court noted that there are no objections in writing and encourages the Parties to talk. Mr. Schauer stated that he is okay with Mr. Lopen's request. Mr. Schauer is to file the inventory and appraisal by the end of this week. A petition for final distribution is to be filed by March 13, 2020. Status on close of estate is continued to April 1, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-25-2019 Continued To Nov-06-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matthew Schauer appeared by telephone for Patricia Costello and Robert Noelke (Executors).
Loren Lopin for Nicholas Kaftan (Attorney-in-Fact for and Son of Dagmar Prudlo sole Beneficiary of this estate).

The Court noted that there is the issue of only one Executor signing the petition, that both Executors are required to sign it, that there is no proof of service conforming to Probate Code 12201(b) and that it is not clear that Mr. Kaftan is able to appear on Ms. Prudlo's behalf as it does not have any papers to support that. Mr. Schauer stated that notice was sent out on September 9th, that the notice of hearing was not filed until Monday (9/23), that his position is that there was sufficient notice and that one of the Executors was on vacation and he could not get a hold of him. Mr. Lopin stated that the Power of Attorney was filed on Monday and was prepared by the same law firm that represents the two Executors. Mr. Schauer stated that he does not contest the Power of Attorney over the Heir. Mr. Lopin stated that he is here because they want some accountability, that one of the properties was sold, that the will called for distribution (not to be sold), that Ms. Costello took a fee for the sale without a Court order, that there are tax penalties and that the estate is bleeding over $10,000.00 a month. Mr. Lopin also stated that there are three other pieces of real estate, that only one person is paying rent and asked the Court not to allow a lengthy period of time. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is continued to November 6, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to obtain a signature from the second Administrator.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
